message:
fix(runtime): CLIENT SETNAME validation, CLIENT LIST TYPE filtering

CLIENT SETNAME: reject any byte <= 0x20 (control characters + space),
not just space. Without this, names containing \n or \r could corrupt
CLIENT LIST output format (which uses \r\n as record separator).

CLIENT SETINFO: same validation for LIB-NAME and LIB-VER fields.

CLIENT LIST TYPE: determine current client type based on actual
subscription state (check pubsub_channel_subs, pubsub_pattern_subs,
pubsub_shard_subs) instead of hardcoding NORMAL=true, PUBSUB=false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
